subject: [PATCH v2 057/110] iomap: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: The patch is a straightforward conversion to PRIino format and does not introduce any new functionality. It appears to be a necessary step in preparation for the widening of i_ino via kino_t.
reviews.raw_body:
Convert iomap i_ino format strings to use the PRIino format
macro in preparation for the widening of i_ino via kino_t.

Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/iomap/ioend.c | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/fs/iomap/ioend.c b/fs/iomap/ioend.c
index 4d1ef8a2cee90b91591d387f8e1c3f75350c1da0..60c4ba5867f8c9edda2d8b14c6cac0e1d0e80484 100644
--- a/fs/iomap/ioend.c
+++ b/fs/iomap/ioend.c
@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@ static u32 iomap_finish_ioend_buffered(struct iomap_ioend *ioend)
 		mapping_set_error(inode->i_mapping, ioend->io_error);
 		if (!bio_flagged(bio, BIO_QUIET)) {
 			pr_err_ratelimited(
-"%s: writeback error on inode %lu, offset %lld, sector %llu",
+"%s: writeback error on inode %" PRIino "u, offset %lld, sector %llu",
 				inode->i_sb->s_id, inode->i_ino,
 				ioend->io_offset, ioend->io_sector);
 		}

-- 
2.53.0


______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch series converts the iomap module to use PRIino format for i_ino, preparing it for the widening of i_ino via kino_t. The change affects various parts of the codebase, including error messages and logging.
